claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 6 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 6 (under 16)
  - Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£0.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child in hospital for 13 weeks but claimant regularly spends on child
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£0.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
circumstances: Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£0.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
Child is not in local authority care
Child in hospital for 13 weeks but claimant regularly spends on child
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'll help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment process.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Let's start by finding out about your child. 

**First question: How old is your child?**

The eligibility depends on your child's age:
- Children under 16 generally qualify automatically (with some conditions)
- Young people aged 16-19 may qualify if they're in approved education or training
- Those aged 20 or over typically don't qualify

Could you tell me your child's age?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi, thanks for helping me with this. I'm asking about my son Alex - he's 6 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So you're asking about your son Alex, who is 6 years old.

Since Alex is under 16, let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect. Now, the next question:

**Is Alex working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?**

Given that Alex is only 6 years old, I assume he's not in paid work at all. Can you confirm that's correct?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's right, he's not working at all - he's just a little kid!

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Of course! That makes perfect sense. Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Next question:

**Is Alex NOT receiving certain benefits in his own right?**

This means benefits like Employment and Support Allowance (ESA) or Universal Credit that would be claimed in his own name. 

Given that Alex is 6 years old, I expect he's not claiming any benefits in his own right. Can you confirm that's correct?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's correct - he doesn't get any benefits in his own name.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! Let me continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Next question:

**Is Alex NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?**

This question is about whether Alex is enrolled in certain types of apprenticeships that would disqualify him from Child Benefit eligibility. However, since Alex is only 6 years old, he wouldn't be in any apprenticeship program.

Can you confirm he's not in any apprenticeship?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not in any apprenticeship - he's far too young for that!

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Absolutely! Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now we need to check your residency status. 

**Do you meet the UK residency requirements for Child Benefit?**

You can qualify if ANY of the following apply to you:

1. **You normally live in the UK** (this is the most common path)
2. You have settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme
3. You have pre-settled status and meet certain conditions (working, studying, job-seeking, or have sufficient resources)
4. You're living abroad as a Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

**Do you normally live in the UK?** This would be the simplest route to qualification.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent! Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Now I need to check if you're responsible for Alex.

**Are you responsible for Alex?**

You can qualify if ANY of the following apply:

1. **You live with Alex** (the most common situation)
2. You contribute to his upkeep (by paying at least the same amount as Child Benefit would be, or equivalent in kind - like food, clothes, housing, pocket money, presents)
3. You pay maintenance under a court order or agreement

**Do you live with Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't live with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I see. Since Alex doesn't live with you, let me check the other ways you can be considered responsible for him.

**Do you contribute to Alex's upkeep?**

This means you regularly pay towards his care - at least the same amount as Child Benefit would be (or equivalent in kind). Contributions can include:
- Money for food, clothes, or pocket money
- Paying for housing costs
- Buying presents
- Other financial support

These can be weekly, monthly, or lump sum payments.

**OR, do you pay maintenance for Alex under a court order or agreement?**

Do either of these situations apply to you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not currently contributing financially to Alex's care.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I understand. Thank you for being honest about your situation.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Based on the information you've provided, I need to deliver the assessment outcome.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Assessment Result: **Not Eligible**

I'm sorry, but based on the information you've provided, **you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex** at this time.

### Why you're not eligible:

To claim Child Benefit, you must be **responsible for the child**. This means you need to either:
- **Live with the child**, OR
- **Pay at least the Child Benefit amount** towards their care (this includes food, clothes, housing, pocket money, presents, etc.), OR
- **Pay maintenance** under a court order or agreement

Since you've confirmed that:
- Alex doesn't live with you, AND
- You're not currently contributing financially to his care

You don't meet the responsibility requirement for claiming Child Benefit.

### What you could do:

If you would like to claim Child Benefit for Alex in the future, you could:
1. **Start contributing financially** to Alex's upkeep - if you pay at least the amount of Child Benefit (or equivalent in kind) regularly towards his care, you could become eligible
2. **Arrange for Alex to live with you** - if circumstances change and Alex comes to live with you

### Who might be eligible instead:

The person who Alex lives with and who is responsible for his day-to-day care would likely be eligible to claim Child Benefit for him (if they meet the other requirements).

If you have any questions about this or your situation changes, please feel free to reach out. You can find more information at: https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/eligibility

reasonings: Alex is 6 years old and meets the age requirement for Child Benefit (under 16). He is not working, not receiving benefits in his own right, and not in an apprenticeship. The claimant meets the UK residency requirements as they live in Manchester. However, the claimant does not meet the responsibility requirement because they do not live with Alex and are not contributing financially to his upkeep. Under Child Benefit rules, you must be responsible for the child, which means either living with them OR paying at least the Child Benefit amount towards their care. Since neither condition is met, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is inquiring about Child Benefit for their 6-year-old son Alex. While Alex qualifies as a child under the age requirements and the claimant meets residency requirements, the claimant does not meet the responsibility requirement as they neither live with Alex nor contribute financially to his upkeep.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent
